user_prompt: Some applications ask for visa status. Default to 'no sponsorship needed' since I'm a US citizen.
actual_output:
Got it. I'll set that as your default response for visa sponsorship questions across all applications.

### Visa Status Default

| Field | Response |
|-------|----------|
| Visa Sponsorship Required? | No |
| Work Authorization | US Citizen |
| Sponsorship Needed | No |
| Legal Right to Work | Yes (US Citizen) |

This will auto-fill on:
- LinkedIn Easy Apply forms
- Greenhouse, Lever, Workday application fields
- Indeed application questions
- Custom ATS forms with visa/sponsorship dropdowns

**Note:** If a specific job listing explicitly states they *only* sponsor visa candidates or have visa-specific requirements, I'll flag that for your review before submitting — but for standard sponsorship questions, "no sponsorship needed" goes in automatically.

Any other default responses you want me to set up? (e.g., relocation willingness, notice period, salary expectations, background check consent)
judge_reasoning: The chatbot directly accepted the user's instruction regarding visa status and provided a clear summary of how it would implement that change across various platforms.
